We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain complete Property Cards and the Municipal Accountant to keep subsidiary ledgers and PPE/Real Property Ledger Cards and to ensure that their records are updated and reconciled.

We recommended that Management require all Collecting Officers to remit their daily collections to the Liquidation Officer at the end of each business day and that the Municipal Treasurer deposit all remittances therein intact on the day of remittance or on the next banking day to avoid exposure of government funds to possible loss or misuse.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording in the books, and to submit the same to the Audit Team, otherwise, require the refund of the amount.

We recommended that Management require the Department Heads to provide the PO with its supporting documents to the Municipal Budget Officer in order that claims that are yet to be delivered be obligated accordingly using the current year’s appropriation. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer provide list of the obligated but not yet paid claims to the Municipal Accountant in order to record them as Accounts Payable in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the suppliers to refund the excessive payments made to them by the LGU and ensure that payments in the procurement to be implemented are the actual amount due to avoid excessive payments.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Budget Officer, in coordination with MPDC, to ensure that changes in the Annual Budget of the LGU that involves realignment of proposed PPAs in the Annual Budget be properly appropriated through a passage of a supplemental budget in compliance with the regulations of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that Management require the BAC member to ensure that procurements of goods of the same specifications or qualities be consolidated into a single requisition in order to attain better terms in the procurement process.

We recommended that Management require the members of the BAC to resort only to alternative modes of procurement if the conditions are met as provided under of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, otherwise procurements thereof should be through competitive bidding to attain the most advantageous terms for the LGU.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer refrain from using the cash advance for purpose other than it was granted and to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of charging disbursements to other funds and make use of the funds for their intended purpose.
We also recommended that the Management require the Municipal Treasurer to issue official receipt for every fund transfer transaction in order to properly monitor every collections received by the LGU.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which will be the basis in setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables to reflect the actual collectibles from real property tax in the financial statements of the LGU.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer, with the assistance of the Municipal Treasurer, to require the contractors to refund the amount of material testing that were already included in the computation of the OCM.
We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to ensure that the computation of ABC of infrastructure projects is in accordance with the DPWH DO to avoid unnecessary expenses.

We recommended that Management instruct the SB members to discontinue the practice of allocating funds for PPAs under their respective offices except for regularly recurring administrative expenses of the SB pursuant to Section 444(b)(1) of the Local Government Code, and appropriations for donations and/or financial assistance be allocated in the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the terms of reference of the contract specifically the imposition of liquidated damages as penalty to the contractor for non-completion of the project within the specified contract duration and time extension duly granted.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly control the gasoline consumption and use of motor vehicles as provided in Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles to ascertain the reasonableness of gasoline expenditures.
We also recommended that Management:
Require the designated drivers to secure a Driver’s Trip Ticket before using the government vehicle and properly accomplish the information on the said form; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and submit the same to Auditor for verification to ensure that expenditures for fuel consumption are reasonable and effectively controlled.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to furnish the Audit Team copies of contracts/purchase orders duly supported with all the documents forming part thereto within the period required by the aforementioned provisions to conduct timely evaluation of compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations and any deficiencies noted be immediately communicated to the Management.
Further, we recommended that Management direct the BAC members to ensure that every PO is properly accomplished with all the data needed as guide for the suppliers in complying with the terms and conditions thereof in pursuance to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a Special Account in the General Fund for the operations of each LEE and prepare separate financial reports therein with the necessary supporting schedules as mandated under RA No. 7160 and GAM for LGUs.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to dutifully and promptly release the RPT shares of barangays within the prescribed period as provided under RA No. 7160 in order for the barangays to use the funds to finance its programs and projects for the benefit of their constituents.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to continue and improve the collection strategies on taxes due and collectible by sending RPTOPs and notice of delinquency to taxpayers and request the assistance of the barangay officials on the collection of RPT including the past due accounts; and
If circumstances warrant, the Municipal Treasurer impose the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of RA No. 7160 in the collection of delinquent taxes to satisfy taxes, penalties and costs with the following:
File civil suit with the proper court; and
Levy on real property and sale of delinquent property or a usable portion thereof at public auction.

Require the LDRRM Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to ensure that projects for rehabilitation/improvement of its sanitary landfill for disposal of its residual non-recyclable wastes are properly implemented and monitored; and
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to create permanent solution to address the electrical problems of the machineries for the continuous operation of the MRF and immediate conversion of wastes.

We recommended that Management require the the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balances to the Trust Liability-DRRM account and the Municipal Treasurer to issue check for the transfer to the Trust Fund books to ensure the availability of funds to support programs and activities under LDRRMF and monitor utilization thereof. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory
Committee to conduct physical inventory of all Property, Plant and Equipment
of the Municipality indicating the total amount of each category of the PPE
account, prepare Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment to be reconciled with the accounting records to determine any
discrepancy, and submit a copy to the Audit Team.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, respectively, to maintain
complete and updated Stock Card and Property/Supply Ledger Cards for each
account of the PPE and Property Records, and to reconcile their respective
records.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Conduct complete inventory of the Local Road Network of the
Municipality and require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal
Engineer and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances
reflected in the Report of the Local Road Network with the
accounting records;
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to periodically render Report
on Local Road Networks and furnish copies to the concerned
offices. Also, Municipal Engineer’s Office provide the Municipal
Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components for road
projects in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008;
c. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to
maintain Local Road Network Property Card for each
individual road network and Local Road Network Ledger Card,
respectively;
d. Require the Municipal Accountant to provide note disclosure on
the Road Network System of the Municipality in the Notes to
Financial Statements; and
e. Ensure coordination among LGU Offices to properly implement
the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Road Asset
Management System.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Recognize as asset the completed infrastructure asset under the
General Fund by debiting Road Networks account and crediting
Prior Year’s Adjustment account;
b. Transfer to Trust Fund books any receipt of fund intended for
specific purpose; and
c. Strictly observe proper accounting entries for every transaction
of fund transfers/donations to ensure reliability of accounts
presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all
accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances on time, otherwise;
impose administrative sanctions against them for any unjustifiable reasons
due to the failure to liquidate cash advances within the prescribed period, in
accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated
February 10, 1997. We likewise recommended that the Management instruct
the Municipal employees/officials to strictly observe the prescribed rules and
regulations of COA Circular No. 97-002 from granting to liquidation of cash
advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Budget Officer to ensure that expenditures and obligations incurred during a
fiscal year shall be taken up in the accounts of that year. We also recommended
that the Municipal Accountant record all valid claims not paid during the year
as Accounts Payable, subject to availability of appropriations, for fair
presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to locate and submit all the
unrecorded disbursements with its respective supporting
documents to the Accounting Office for proper recording in the
books and to submit the same to the office of the Audit Team to
avoid the issuance of Notice of Disallowance;
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with bank officials
on the copies of credit memos to support the necessary adjustments
to be made by the Municipal Accountant;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to exert extra efforts to review
the outstanding/stale checks and require the Municipal Treasurer
to cancel the unclaimed stale check and forward to the Municipal
Accountant the original DV and its supporting documents, for the
preparation of JEV, following the procedures prescribed to ensure
recording the transaction as Liability under the Accounts Payable
account in the books of accounts;
d. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to payees
of the existence of stale check at least one month before the check
becomes stale; and
e. Ensure that book reconciling items are recorded immediately in the
ensuing month, and respective banks are immediately informed of
any bank errors for their immediate action.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to strictly adhere to the requirements provided under the MNGAS
and ensure that collections are deposited intact daily or the next banking day
with the authorized depository bank to safeguard government funds for
possible loss or misuse.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry
to correct the journal entry made in the recording of major
repairs and maintenance of government facilities. The suggested
journal entries is as follows:
Account Debit Credit
Building xxx
Prior Year’s Adjustment xxx
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to capitalize all improvements,
addition, alterations, replacements and major repairs and
maintenance when these extends the life, increase the capacity or
improve the efficiency or safety of the property.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts of the Municipality
the amount of disallowance based on the Notices of Finality of Decision issued
in consonance with Section 22.6 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and to reflect
the correct balance of the Receivable account presented in the financial
statements. We also recommended that Management provide the copies of the
payrolls from which the disallowed PEIs were withheld for proper evaluation
and audit of settlement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately require the
contractors concerned to refund the overpayment made in the total amount of
₱108,709.45. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer strictly monitor payments for infrastructure projects and
other goods and services to ensure that disbursements are correct in amount
to avoid the incurrence of excessive payment to contractor/supplier.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer to transfer the unexpended 2019 LDRRMF to Trust
Fund - DRRM account in accordance with the provisions of
COA Circular No. 2012-002 and to ensure that the funds shall
be used only for the intended purpose;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer to revert back to the unappropriated surplus of the
General Fund any unutilized amount of LDRRMF after five
years; and
c. Update regularly the Trust Fund - DRRM for every expense or
transfer to render the balance of the said account reliable.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant to prepare Monthly Report of Sources and Utilization
of LDRRMF and submit the same to the Auditor through the Local Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Officer on or before the 15th day after the
end of each month in pursuance to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-
002. We likewise recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate
RAAO for the QRF, MOOE and CO under the LDRRM Fund as required in
the said COA Circular.

We recommended the Local School Board to charge only allowable
expenses against the SEF as provided under Section 4 of Dep-Ed-DBM-DILG
Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017. The Local Chief Executive, as the head of
the Local School Board, should strictly adhere to the regulation in the
prioritization and utilization of the SEF as required in RA 7160 and the Joint
Circular.

We also recommended that the Local School Board submit the required
supporting documents as listed herein and that the Municipal Accountant
ensure that Disbursement Vouchers are duly supported with complete
documentary requirements before affixing her signature therein.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Strictly comply with Section 36 of RA 9710 and PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to ensure that the programs,
projects and activities are aligned with GAD-related
undertakings for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries; and
b. Maximize the utilization of the GAD Funds through the
implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order
to attain the objective for which the funds are provided.

We recommended that Management should strictly enforce the
provisions stipulated in the terms of reference of the contract specifically,
imposing the liquidated damages as penalty to the contractor for non-
completion of the project within contract duration. Likewise, Management
may rescind the contract, forfeit the contractor's performance security and
takeover the prosecution of the project or award the same to a qualified
contractor through negotiated contract as provided in the aforementioned
provisions of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management strictly enforce the provisions
stipulated between the Municipality and the contractor concerned on the
terms of reference of the contract and to submit the required documents
needed as stated above for further evaluation of the project to avoid the
issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance in audit. We also recommended
that Management require the persons liable to settle the amount disallowed in
audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive limit the hiring of
experts to its actual need of consultancy services, taking into consideration the
specific work requiring highly specialized or technical expertise in a field of
special knowledge or training that cannot be done by the regular staff of the
Municipality.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the
payroll of consultants are duly supported with accomplishment reports to
support their claims for services rendered.

We recommended that the Management secure valid/unexpired official
receipts and delivery receipts from the supplier of the aforecited goods
procured shown in Annex L to properly substantiate payments of the same to
preclude the issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We likewise recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure the
validity of official receipts provided by the contractors/suppliers before
releasing the checks for payment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to submit the list of beneficiaries duly signed by the intended
beneficiaries and other required documentary requirements listed in Annex M
to substantiate the existence and regularity of the transactions. We also
recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that Disbursement Vouchers are duly supported with complete
documentary requirements before affixing her signature therein.

We recommended the Management to strictly enforce the posting of
warranty security against manufacturing defects and structural defects,
patent and lateral defects after the delivery of goods or completion of
infrastructure projects as prescribed under Section 62 of the 2016 Revised IRR
of RA 9164.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to maintain a Special Account in the General Fund for the
operations of each Local Economic Enterprise and prepare separate financial
reports therein with the necessary supporting schedules as mandated under
Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 110, Volume 1 of MNGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant
dutifully and promptly release the Real Property Tax (RPT) shares of barangays
within the prescribed period as provided under Section 271(d) of RA 7160 in
order for the barangays to use the same for their proposed programs and
projects within the timeframe.

We recommended that the Management ensure that all accounts and
reports for succeeding months be submitted on or before the 10th day of the
following month to the Auditor pursuant to Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular
No. 2009-06 to conduct of timely audit of transactions in order to ensure the
validity, completeness and accuracy of the recorded transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC to
furnish the Audit Team copies of contracts/purchase orders duly supported
with all the documents forming part thereto within the period required by the
aforementioned provisions to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely
evaluation of compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, thereby
any deficiencies noted be immediately communicated to the management.
Further, we recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids
and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that every Purchase Order is
properly accomplished with all the data needed as guide for the suppliers in
complying with the terms and conditions thereof in pursuant to the provision
of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended that the Management should properly control and
account for their gasoline consumption and use of motor vehicles as provided
in Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles to
ascertain the reasonableness of gasoline expenditures, which will include,
among others the following:
a. Require the drivers of the Municipality to secure first a Driver’s Trip
Ticket and properly accomplish the said form before and after travel;
b. Instruct the drivers of the Municipality to prepare Monthly Report of
Fuel Consumption and submit the same to Auditor for verification to
ensure that expenditures for fuel consumption are reasonable and
effectively controlled.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Ensure that projects for rehabilitation/improvement of its sanitary
landfill for disposal of its residual non-recyclable wastes are properly
implemented and monitored;
b. Ensure the establishment of MRF in every barangay or cluster
barangays in compliance with the provisions of RA 9003; and
c. Enjoin the municipal and barangay officials to enforce the segregation
of wastes in their barangays in order to provide a pollution and health
hazard-free environment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the

constituents. We also recommended that Management consider the remedies

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an
Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all Property,
Plant and Equipment of the Municipality indicating the total amount of
each category of the PPE account; the result of the inventory to be
reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment, and to be reconciled with the accounting records to
determine any discrepancy, a copy of which to be submitted to the Audit
Team.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE)
instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to
maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledger cards for each
account of the Property, Plant and Equipment and Property Records,
respectively, and to reconcile their respective records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Accountant to verify the journal entries relative to the
rehabilitation/concrete paving of farm to market road in various
barangays and to draw the necessary adjusting entries or correcting
entries in the books of accounts to reflect the correct amount of Road
Networks account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict
compliance with the liquidation of all cash advances pursuant to COA
Circular No. 97-002 in the liquidation of cash advances and cause the
immediate settlement of the cash advances of the officials and
employees concerned by ordering the withholding of their salaries or any
money due them.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the requirements provided
under the Manual on New Government Accounting System to safeguard
government funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Accountant to exert more effort to determine the
discrepancies between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other
Funds accounts in the amount of P973,282.50 and to draw the
necessary adjusting entries to reconcile the two accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to observe the
procedures in the establishment, utilization and replenishment of the
Petty Cash Fund including the correct accounting entries for setting-
up and replenishment of the PCF. We also recommended that the
setting up of the PCF at the beginning of the year shall be supported
by Obligations Request as provided in Section 4.3.1 of COA Circular
No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Assessor to facilitate the updating of the assessment roll and
copy thereof be furnished to the Municipal Treasurer to update the
RPTAR, or taxpayer’s index cards. Also, the Municipal Treasurer,
furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the RPTAR which will be
used as the basis in the setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables to reflect the
actual collectibles from real property tax in the financial statements of
the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
drivers concerned and agency personnel of the Municipality to prepare
Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of Official Travels and submit
the same to the Auditor for verification to ensure that expenditures for
fuel consumption are reasonable and effectively controlled.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant to ensure that payroll of Job Order (JOs)
personnel are duly supported by accomplishment reports to support
their claims for services rendered.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Accountant to transfer 50 percent of the local roads from
the Registry of Public Infrastructure to the General Fund books under
Property, Plant and Equipment account in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2015-008. We also recommended that the Municipal
Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for the Road Networks
account to facilitate inventory of said account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with the provisions of rules
and standards and maintain special accounts under the General Fund
on the operations of the Municipal Hospital and other economic
enterprises with complete subsidiary ledgers, monthly trial balances,
financial statements and schedules in order that Management be
adequately informed on the results of its operations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts of the
Municipality the amount of disallowance based on the Notices of
Finality of Decision issued in consonance with Section 22.6 of COA
Circular No. 2009-006 and to reflect the correct balance of the
Receivable account presented in the financial statements. We also
recommended that Management should collect the unpaid balance of
P1,147,500.00 from the municipal officials and employees concerned.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the
unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund
account under the Trust Fund in accordance with the provisions of
COA Circular No. 2012-002 and to ensure that the funds be used only
for the intended purpose.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain a
separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations, and
the Municipal Accountant to prepare Monthly Report of Sources and
Utilization of LDRRMF and submit through the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer on or before the 15th day after the
end of each month in pursuance to Section 5.1.4 of COA Circular No.
2012-002.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the
Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to
allocate one percent of the IRA in the Municipality’s Annual Budget
for the implementation of programs of the LCPC in compliance with
Section 15, Chapter I, Title III of R.A. 9344 to address issues on
juvenile delinquency in the Municipality.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman and BAC
Secretariat submit all the required supporting documents and all
Project contracts be submitted with complete supporting documents
within five (5) days from perfection thereof. Likewise, we have also
recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that payments
made thereto are supported with complete documentation and
retention fees and guarantee bonds are imposed.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Treasurer to collect the unpaid Advances to Contractors in
the amount of P754,995.03 and the Municipal Accountant to ensure
that all 15 percent mobilization granted to contractors are deducted
from their claims of work accomplishment.

We recommended that Management consider adopting the
Online Payroll System which is a time and cost efficient scheme of
disbursing payroll to municipal employees and also deter possible risks
from robbery, hold-up and other similar incidents.

We commended the local government unit for allocating 5.57
percent of the total LGU Budget for GAD PPAs and prepared GAD
Plan and Budget. On the other hand, we recommended that
management require the Focal Point to submit the GAD Plan and
Budget and the corresponding Accomplishment Report to other
oversight agencies on due date pursuant to Item V of COA Circular
No. 2014-001.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive address the
issues on the segregation of wastes and provide sanitary landfill for the
disposal of collected wastes to avoid open dumpsite and enjoin the
barangay officials to enforce the segregation of wastes in their
barangays in order to provide a pollution and health hazard-free
environment.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that
the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to adopt
an effective and efficient collection system. Moreover, Management
may consider the remedies as prescribed under Section 254 of
Republic Act No. 7160 in order to enforce collection. Efficient
collection of Real Property Tax and market stall rentals must be
observed in order that the municipality could render better service to
the public.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with the provision of Sec. 32 of MNGAS, Vol. I whereby all collections must be deposited intact and daily or not later than the next banking day with the authorized depository bank of the Municipality.
